Asset description: AI converts BRD/SOW documents into structured epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria for LOS, LMS, SME, Mortgage and Cards projects.
Business Benefits / Impact: 40-60% documentation effort reduction and improved traceability.
Implementation Guidelines: Upload BRD/SOW; AI generates structured backlog aligned with templates.
Strategic Takeaways: Standardizes requirement quality across BFSI tower.
Remarks / Additonal Comments / Support n: Requires secure document environment.
Validation Notes: Strong BFSI BA accelerator. AI converts BRD/SOW into epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria for LOS/LMS/SME/Mortgage/Cards. Effort from 8–10 MD to 3–4 MD (40–60% reduction). Named owner (BA Lead – BFSI), tool confirmed (Copilot). No blocking gaps.
Reality / Doability: High feasibility. BRD-to-user-story conversion is well-proven. Financial domain specificity (LOS, Mortgage) requires BFSI-aware prompt context. Risk: AI-generated acceptance criteria may miss regulatory and compliance constraints specific to banking. Mitigation: BFSI BA lead reviews all generated stories against regulatory requirements before backlog entry.
Suggestion for How to apply: 1) Upload BRD/SOW (sanitized). 2) Provide BFSI context prompt (domain: LOS/Mortgage, regulatory constraints, acceptance criteria standards). 3) AI generates: epics, user stories, acceptance criteria, dependency notes. 4) BA Lead reviews against regulatory requirements. 5) Approved stories enter JIRA/ADO backlog. 6) Pilot: one LOS or Mortgage project BRD. 7) KPI: MD reduction per project; story quality score from delivery team.
Why this case updated: Accepted – Ready for Pilot

Asset description: AI generates architecture diagrams, integration flows, sequence diagrams and NFR checklist from BRD and integration inputs.
Business Benefits / Impact: 30-50% faster design cycle and reduced architecture review iterations.
Implementation Guidelines: Provide structured requirements and integration inventory.
Strategic Takeaways: Improves governance and architectural consistency.
Remarks / Additonal Comments / Support n: Needs predefined templates.
Validation Notes: Strong architecture design accelerator. AI generates architecture diagrams, integration flows, sequence diagrams, and NFR checklists from BRD and integration inputs. Effort from 12–15 MD to 6–8 MD (30–50% reduction). Named owner (Solution Architecture Lead), tool confirmed (Copilot). Pilot: one integration-heavy BFSI program.
Reality / Doability: High feasibility for diagram and documentation drafting. Risk: AI may propose inappropriate patterns for regulated banking environments. Mitigation: mandatory Architecture Review Board sign-off on all AI-generated architecture before baselining.
Suggestion for How to apply: 1) Provide BRD and integration inventory (systems, APIs, data flows). 2) AI generates: HLD diagram, integration blueprint, sequence diagrams, NFR checklist. 3) Solution Architect reviews against banking environment constraints. 4) Architecture Review Board sign-off. 5) Baseline approved artifacts. 6) Pilot: one integration-heavy BFSI program. 7) KPI: design cycle time before vs after; review iterations needed.
Why this case updated: Accepted – Ready for Pilot

Asset description: AI analyzes rate cards, MD allocation, and burn rate to predict margin impact and recommend resource optimization across towers.
Business Benefits / Impact: 5-8% margin improvement and 10-15% resource optimization.
Implementation Guidelines: Integrate PAS sheets and costing catalogues.
Strategic Takeaways: Protects profitability while improving delivery efficiency.
Remarks / Additonal Comments / Support n: Requires access to financial sheets.
Validation Notes: Strong delivery excellence use case. AI analyzes rate cards, MD allocation, and burn rate to predict margin impact and recommend resource optimization. Outcome: 5–8% margin improvement, 10–15% resource optimization. Named owner (Delivery Management Head), tool confirmed (Copilot). Pilot: 2–3 accounts. No blocking gaps.
Reality / Doability: High feasibility for margin modeling and optimization recommendations. Risk: AI recommendations may not account for contractual constraints or client-relationship implications of resource changes. Mitigation: Delivery Management Head reviews and approves all resource optimization actions before execution.
Suggestion for How to apply: 1) Integrate PAS sheets and costing catalogues as AI input. 2) AI analyzes: rate card vs allocation, burn rate trends, margin variance per project. 3) AI generates: margin impact forecast, over/under-allocation flags, optimization recommendations. 4) Delivery Management Head reviews recommendations. 5) Approved actions implemented. 6) Pilot: 2–3 accounts. 7) KPI: margin % improvement; resource utilization before vs after.
Why this case updated: Accepted – Ready for Pilot

Asset Name: AI-Augmented Oracle HCM Cloud Implementation Strategy
Asset description:
This proposal introduces a Knowledge-Based Agent System that serves as an institutional expert across all Oracle HCM implementation phases. The system comprises two foundational components:
•	Centralized Knowledge Repository: A structured database containing historical project documentation (BRDs, fit-gap analyses, meeting minutes, MoSCoW prioritizations, future-state process maps, solution designs, technical specifications) and verified government policies from official sources (labor regulations, social insurance requirements, taxation rules, localization standards). Documents are tagged with metadata including industry vertical, public/private sector classification, implemented modules, and country-specific requirements.
•	AI Agent Layer: A generative AI system trained on the knowledge repository, capable of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), natural language interaction, document drafting, policy analysis, and predictive risk assessment. The agent operates through defined workflows aligned with implementation methodology phases.
Business Benefits / Impact:
•	Time saved (estimated): 40% faster project delivery.
•	Effort reduced: 30-50% reduction in documentation workload.
•	Quality improvement: 40% fewer defects, 60% improved policy compliance, 35% better requirement completeness.
•	Reduction in errors or rework.
Implementation Guidelines:
The system leverages vector database for semantic similarity matching. Documents undergo preprocessing: text extraction, embedding generation using OpenAI or Oracle Cohere models, and metadata tagging.
The AI agent utilizes a large language model (Oracle Generative AI, Azure OpenAI GPT-4, or AWS) configured for retrieval-augmented generation. When queried, the system retrieves top relevant document chunks, injects them as context into the prompt, and generates responses grounded in organizational knowledge.
Strategic Takeaways:
Faster kickoff, reduced policy research effort, improved proposal quality.

Faster fit-gap completion, better solution consistency, improved requirement coverage.

Faster configuration setup, fewer errors, quicker data mapping.

Asset description:
using AI to optimize the SQL queries to remove full table scan.
In some of our PL/SQL code (SQL Queries), we are getting Full table scan. So in all such cases if the table is very huge, then the query performance is getting very slow and the execution time is very high which results in poor performance. So we need to optimize the code for to achieve better performance
Business Benefits / Impact: 80% reduction in time
Implementation Guidelines:
1. Identifying the query that needs to optimized using the Execution plan in Toad or SQL Developer.
2. Clearly defining the joining conditions of different tables being used in the query.
3. Building indexes on the different columns being used on the joinging conditions.
4. taking help of AI to optimize the code
Strategic Takeaways: Getting Optimized SQL Queries without full table scan
Tools / Platform Used: Toad for Oracle, Copilot for taking help
Validation Notes: Well-formed use case with clear business value, valid AI role, and enough delivery context to move into controlled implementation with SME review and quality gates.
Reality / Doability: High feasibility. The use case has a clear business problem, a valid AI role, and a workable delivery path if grounded on approved inputs and reviewed by the responsible SME/lead before release.

2) Track coverage improvement, preparation effort, and escaped defects.
3) Reuse validated scenarios/prompts inside the QE repository.
No. of Hours Saved with each Implementat: It will help us reduce the development time by at least 30%
